Tom Owen:

I'm aware of media reports alleging CX Director of Flight Operations, Chris Kempis, of hitting a protester today in Tung Chung with his car, and subsequently slamming the car door, injuring a reporter.

As a Hong Kong citizen I am strongly appalled by this incident. A senior Cathay Pacific manager blatantly flouting the law and endangering all others around him is a poor, but sadly fair and accurate manifest of the ethics of your company.

I must also add that under your reign, Cathay staff are experiencing a Cultural Revolution style storm with your team actively encouraging employees to inform on one another. Not only are you spearheading the demoralisation of the business sector, you are adversely affecting the well being of many staff members, their families and the general public. I trust you understand that should there be any deaths or serious injuries as a direct or indirect result of your company's policy or initiatives, Hong Kong people shall hold you and CX top management accountable.

I am genuinely confounded as to how you and the rest of CX top management sleep at night

Sincerely,
你個大名

Not only are you spearheading the demoralisation of the business sector, you are adversely affecting the well being of many staff members, their families and the general public. I trust you understand that should there be any deaths or serious injuries as a direct or indirect result of your company's policy or initiatives, Hong Kong people shall hold you and CX top management accountable.

I am genuinely confounded as to how you and the rest of CX top management sleep at night

Sincerely,
你個大名

國泰已經完全親共
